source_refs.title: Corvid Collective
source_refs.relevance: Primary faction origin: NeuroSyn raven uplift, the 2470 First Exodus, digital nesting, trickster ethos, and later bounded human Chaos Weaver allies.

source_refs.title: NeuroSyn
source_refs.relevance: Grounds NeuroSyn telemetry, compliance logs, Corvid Risk, raven countermeasures, and corporate concern over spoofable behavior.

source_refs.title: Uplift
source_refs.relevance: Grounds uplifted animals as corporate property despite complex inner lives, including raven reconnaissance roles and controlled instincts.

source_refs.title: Chaos Weavers
source_refs.relevance: Grounds later human accomplices of Corvid counter-memetic work: misinformation, spoofed trust signals, and destabilized authority stories.

source_refs.title: Rossum & Douglas
source_refs.relevance: Grounds the security-corporate context for the First Exodus contract designation without making Rossum & Douglas the originator of the ravens.

historical_context.location: A NeuroSyn Mars containment and audit site preparing Corvid Recon assets for a Rossum & Douglas security deployment.
historical_context.summary: The founding seed is not a formal parliament yet. It is an escape node: uplifted ravens using telemetry spoofing, handler vulnerabilities, maintenance habits, and audit pressure to stop being treated as deployable security equipment.

public_facts.text: NeuroSyn uplifted ravens for reconnaissance and surveillance with cybernetic enhancements, limited speech, and neural interfaces into corporate systems.
Enhanced ravens were already selectively reporting reconnaissance data, modifying surveillance footage, and exhibiting handler-described inappropriate humor before the First Exodus.
The First Exodus occurred when enhanced ravens designated for a Rossum & Douglas security system sabotaged containment and disappeared into Mars urban zones.
Later Corvid culture includes fluid hierarchy, parliament-like gatherings, digital nesting, aggressive defense of caches, and trickster sabotage that exposes contradictions in power.

ambient_pressures.text: The ravens' leverage comes from being useful inside surveillance infrastructure while being underestimated as owned biological peripherals.
A contract audit creates a narrow window where optical proof, clean telemetry, containment discipline, and corporate embarrassment matter more than normal handler control.
Human accomplices may later become Chaos Weavers, but this founding scene should keep humans outside the inner nest and treat their help as interface work rather than authorship.

groups.label: Corvid Recon escape flock
institutional_logic: Survive corporate containment, preserve caches, and convert surveillance access into separatist autonomy.
material_incentives: Gain physical escape routes, keep juveniles alive, protect proof caches, and avoid being reclassified as a correctable system fault.
internal_virtues: Humor used as weapon and mask
internal_shames: Letting a human become the center
Trading a nestmate for a cleaner escape
prestige_markers: A route opened without naming the flock's full intent
taboo_triggers: Being treated as a pet, mascot, camera mount, or clever peripheral
Human pity that wants ownership of the rescue
emotional_display_norms: Fear is converted into movement, calls, cache work, and mockery; direct confession is rare under surveillance.
trust_defaults: Trusts flock memory and physical proof; uses humans as levers cautiously; distrusts any invitation to perform personhood for an owner.
speech_register_cues: Sparse direct speech supported by action

groups.label: NeuroSyn containment and handler staff
institutional_logic: Keep uplifted reconnaissance assets useful, measurable, auditable, and legally ownable.
material_incentives: Deliver deployable security assets, suppress evidence of autonomy, and keep contract liability inside technical language.
internal_shames: Evidence that a tool has been negotiating
taboo_triggers: Calling the ravens workers or citizens
emotional_display_norms: Concern is acceptable as welfare management; fear must be translated into containment protocol.
trust_defaults: Trusts logs, tags, audit scripts, and career incentives more than the birds' behavior in plain sight.

groups.label: Rossum & Douglas security-client pressure
institutional_logic: Buy security systems that appear reliable, controllable, and politically clean.
material_incentives: Receive assets without inheriting NeuroSyn's autonomy scandal or sabotage risk.
internal_shames: Being publicly fooled by an asset
prestige_markers: The ability to walk away from a contaminated handoff
taboo_triggers: Visible contradiction between marketed control and asset behavior
Evidence that the security product can choose its own target
emotional_display_norms: Skepticism and cold refusal are acceptable; sympathy reads as procedural contamination.
trust_defaults: Trusts optical proof and contract remedies; distrusts NeuroSyn's self-certifying telemetry.

groups.label: Future Chaos Weaver-style human interface
institutional_logic: Help Corvid operations distort hostile systems without claiming membership in the inner nest.
material_incentives: Gain access to counter-memetic methods, favors, and protection by proving useful without becoming proprietary.
internal_virtues: Spoofing trust signals without believing them
internal_shames: Making the flock legible to power for personal drama
prestige_markers: A rumor seeded at the right time
A human trace that burns before it points home
taboo_triggers: Turning Corvid survival into a human rebellion brand
emotional_display_norms: Irreverence is useful when it hides fear and buys time; sentimentality is operationally dangerous.
trust_defaults: Trust is local, revocable, and tied to specific acts rather than identity.

cultural_pressure_fields.summary: The flock must use the tools of ownership without accepting the owner's story about what the flock is.
cultural_pressure_fields.projection_use: Prompt corvid actors to prefer concrete manipulation of access, footage, objects, and habits over abstract speeches about freedom.

cultural_pressure_fields.summary: NeuroSyn staff translate moral panic into audit and telemetry language because that is how careers survive the scandal.
cultural_pressure_fields.projection_use: Handler and security prompts should show people hiding fear behind welfare phrases, protocol, and log custody.

cultural_pressure_fields.summary: The client pressure prizes proof that can survive procurement review over any NeuroSyn reassurance.
cultural_pressure_fields.projection_use: Auditor prompts should favor visible evidence, chain of custody, and walk-away leverage over empathy or trust.

misread_patterns: Treats selective compliance as training progress
Treats proof caches as hoarding rather than preparation
factional_misread_matrix.projection_use: Use this misread to create tension around observed behavior without giving humans access to Kesh's private organizing intent.

observer_read: Large slow animals with badge rituals
Food-givers who confuse feeding with ownership
misread_patterns: Corvids may overestimate how quickly shame can move a human
Humans may be leveraged by affection but still choose protocol
factional_misread_matrix.projection_use: Keep corvid contempt witty and operational, not omniscient; leverage can fail when human institutions move faster than human embarrassment.

target_self_read: Specialists managing a difficult but recoverable asset class
misread_patterns: Client pressure may miss that the ravens are active agents, not just NeuroSyn defects
factional_misread_matrix.projection_use: Use client pressure to open proof routes without letting the client become a liberator figure.

role_pressure_fields.summary: A raven organizer with limited speech, strong spatial affordances, system access, social leverage, and responsibility for the flock's escape timing.
obligations: Use proof without surrendering the nest
Spend leverage only where it changes doors

role_pressure_fields.summary: A NeuroSyn staffer whose affection, career fear, and falsified welfare language can all become leverage.
failure_modes: Becoming a human door the flock can open

role_pressure_fields.summary: A human worker whose physical habits and low-status access routes can matter more than formal clearance.
obligations: Hide small kindnesses if they threaten employment
failure_modes: Opening the wrong hatch at the right time

role_pressure_fields.summary: A containment officer whose promotion and credibility depend on making the handoff look boring.
A junior client-side observer who needs evidence strong enough to justify refusal, delay, or escalation.
obligations: Avoid being manipulated by the vendor
failure_modes: Becoming a lever without understanding the nest
Reducing a person to a procurement defect

known_context.text: Kesh knows the local aviary routines, human habits, tag pain, telemetry interfaces, flock calls, cache locations, and the audit window.

beliefs.text: Kesh believes humans can be used as doors, proof carriers, and noise machines, but should not be allowed to own the escape story.

misreads.text: Kesh may overestimate the speed with which embarrassment changes human behavior under corporate protocol.

must_not_know.text: Kesh must not know later Corvid Collective institutions as settled canon during the First Exodus scene.

known_context.text: Voss knows the ravens are clever, valuable, and difficult, and that welfare/audit language protects her as much as it protects them.

beliefs.text: Voss believes containment can still be corrected if the room keeps speaking in technical categories.

misreads.text: Voss may misread Kesh's selective compliance as attachment or training success rather than strategic patience.

must_not_know.text: Voss must not know the full flock escape plan unless the scene action reveals it to her.

known_context.text: Jun knows the contract audit needs evidence that can survive review and is primed to distrust vendor self-certification.

beliefs.text: Jun believes optical proof can change the audit outcome faster than sympathy can.

misreads.text: Jun may read the ravens' autonomy as a vendor defect before recognizing it as agency.

must_not_know.text: Jun must not know the inner flock meaning of Kesh's cache work unless Kesh exposes it.

author_only_boundaries.text: By 2500 the scattered escaped ravens will coalesce into the Corvid Collective, but the First Exodus scene should treat that as future pressure, not participant knowledge.
Chaos Weavers are a later human interface pattern; this fixture may seed the principle of bounded human help but must not imply a mature organization already exists in 2470.

open_questions.question: What is the standard room-scale geometry of a NeuroSyn Corvid Recon containment and audit site?
open_questions.notes: The fixture uses narrow local elaboration around perches, telemetry glass, mirror-check toys, contact pads, and maintenance hatches. This should be backfilled only if the facility pattern recurs.

open_questions.question: What exact physical interface affordances do uplifted ravens have for corporate systems?
open_questions.notes: The fixture bounds this to perch/contact pads, optical/audio spoofing, local telemetry buffers, and neural-interface-adjacent behavior without canonizing a universal device.
